title: IDE To Filesystem Server
scenario: An AI coding IDE acts as the host, creates an MCP client, and connects to a filesystem MCP server so it can inspect project files.
what_to_notice: The host is not the server. The host creates a client connection to a separate capability provider.

title: One Host Many Servers
scenario: A desktop AI app connects to separate search, notes, and database MCP servers without inventing a different integration style for each one.
what_to_notice: MCP reduces bespoke glue code by giving hosts a standard way to connect to many external systems.

title: Capability Negotiation
scenario: A host starts a session with an MCP server and learns which features the server supports before attempting tool calls.
what_to_notice: MCP is stateful. Initialization and capability negotiation happen before normal operation.

title: Resources For Context
scenario: A company exposes internal documentation through an MCP server so the host can fetch reliable context when answering user questions.
what_to_notice: Resources provide context, while tools perform actions.

title: Tool Invocation
scenario: A host calls a server tool to search logs, read a file, or query a system, then uses the result inside the model workflow.
what_to_notice: Tools enable actions, not just passive context retrieval.
